questions.question: What is a force?
questions.answer: A force is a push or a pull acting on an object.

questions.question: Name one effect of force.
questions.answer: Force can change the motion of an object.

questions.question: What happens when we push a door?
questions.answer: The door moves due to the applied force.

questions.question: Give one example of a pull force.
questions.answer: Pulling a bucket from a well is an example of pull force.

questions.question: What is friction?
questions.answer: Friction is a force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact.

questions.question: What type of force is friction?
questions.answer: Friction is a contact force.

questions.question: Name one contact force.
questions.answer: Muscular force is a contact force.

questions.question: Name one non-contact force.
questions.answer: Gravitational force is a non-contact force.

questions.question: Why does an apple fall to the ground?
questions.answer: It falls due to gravitational force.

questions.question: What is gravitational force?
questions.answer: Gravitational force is the force that pulls objects toward the Earth.

questions.question: What is magnetic force?
questions.answer: Magnetic force is the force by which a magnet attracts or repels objects.

questions.question: Give an example of magnetic force.
questions.answer: A magnet attracting iron nails.

questions.question: What happens when force is applied to a moving object?
questions.answer: Its speed or direction can change.

questions.question: Can force change the shape of an object?
questions.answer: Yes, force can change the shape of an object.

questions.question: Give an example where force changes shape.
questions.answer: Stretching a rubber band changes its shape.

questions.question: What is muscular force?
questions.answer: Muscular force is the force applied by muscles of living beings.

questions.question: Why is friction useful?
questions.answer: Friction helps us walk without slipping.

questions.question: Why do vehicles have brakes?
questions.answer: Brakes use friction to stop vehicles.

questions.question: What happens if there is no friction?
questions.answer: Objects would keep sliding and it would be difficult to walk.

questions.question: Name one force that acts without touching objects.
questions.answer: Magnetic force.

questions.question: What type of force pulls objects toward Earth?
questions.answer: Gravitational force.

questions.question: What does force do to stationary objects?
questions.answer: Force can make them move.

questions.question: What does force do to moving objects?
questions.answer: Force can stop or change their direction.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of force?
questions.answer: The SI unit of force is newton (N).

questions.question: What instrument is used to measure force?
questions.answer: A spring balance is used to measure force.
